openmeter/billing/charges/usagebased/ser.purpose: Stateless computation sub-package that snapshots metered usage from ClickHouse and converts it into rated detailed lines or totals via the parent billing rating service. No DB writes — all persistence is handled by callers.

patterns.name: Config-struct constructor with Validate()
patterns.description: New(Config) validates all required fields before returning a Service. Every exported input type also implements Validate().
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (Service, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err } ... }

patterns.name: StoredAtOffset cutoff in meter queries
patterns.description: All ClickHouse queries use FilterStoredAt.Lt = &StoredAtOffset so usage is bounded to a deterministic point in time, enabling idempotent re-rating.

patterns.name: Service-period-scoped ChildUniqueReferenceID
patterns.description: After rating, every DetailedLine.ChildUniqueReferenceID is suffixed with the UTC service period via withServicePeriodInDetailedLineChildUniqueReferenceIDs to guarantee global uniqueness across reruns.

patterns.name: Prefer GetTotalsForUsage over GetDetailedLinesForUsage when only totals needed
patterns.description: GetTotalsForUsage skips detailed-line construction, making it faster. Both methods share the same snapshotQuantity helper.

patterns.name: PriorRuns must have DetailedLines expanded before rating
patterns.description: GetDetailedLinesForUsageInput.Validate() checks that all PriorRuns have DetailedLines.IsPresent(); callers must pre-fetch via adapter.FetchDetailedLines.
patterns.example: if !run.DetailedLines.IsPresent() { return fmt.Errorf("prior runs[%d]: detailed lines must be expanded") }

key_file_guides.role: Defines the Service interface (GetTotalsForUsage, GetDetailedLinesForUsage), Config struct, and New constructor.
key_file_guides.watch_for: This is a pure computation service — no Ent/DB dependency; never add adapter or tx logic here.

key_file_guides.role: Implements GetDetailedLinesForUsage: snapshots quantity, calls ratingService.GenerateDetailedLines, rewrites ChildUniqueReferenceIDs.
key_file_guides.watch_for: IgnoreMinimumCommitment flag must be threaded as a billingrating.WithMinimumCommitmentIgnored() option — not a conditional branch.

key_file_guides.role: Implements GetTotalsForUsage: same snapshot + rating path but extracts only ratingResult.Totals.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Does NOT suffix ChildUniqueReferenceIDs — do not add that logic here.

key_file_guides.role: Private snapshotQuantity helper that queries ClickHouse via streaming.Connector.QueryMeter and sums all rows.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Validation error wrapped as billing.ValidationError{Err: err} — use the same pattern for new validations.
Timestamps formatted with time.RFC3339 in UTC; changing format breaks deduplication of existing persisted lines.

key_file_guides.role: Unit tests using streamingtestutils.NewMockStreamingConnector and a stub ratingService — no DB required.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Tests use t.Context(); never substitute context.Background().

anti_patterns: Adding Ent/DB adapter calls inside this package — all persistence is handled by callers in the run package.
Calling streaming.Connector with a nil StoredAtOffset — every query must respect the stored-at cutoff.
Skipping ChildUniqueReferenceID suffixing when adding new detailed-line paths — persisted lines will collide across reruns.
Returning mutable slices from snapshotQuantity without copying — summarizeMeterQueryRow already returns a new value, keep it immutable.
Bypassing Config.Validate() or input.Validate() before use — all constructors/methods must validate first.

decisions.decision: Stateless package with no DB dependency
decisions.rationale: Rating is a pure computation: snapshot usage + apply rate card. Keeping it DB-free makes it trivially testable with mock connectors and reusable from multiple callers without transaction concerns.

The same rate-card key (e.g. 'unit-price-usage') is reused across multiple billing periods. Appending the UTC period in RFC3339 makes each child reference globally unique so upsert logic in the run package can safely idempotently re-persist lines.

code_examples.scenario: Rate a usage-based charge and retrieve detailed lines with the stored-at cutoff

openmeter/billing/charges/usagebased/ser.purpose: Owns the mechanics of usage-based realization runs: creating rated runs, persisting detailed lines, allocating/correcting credits, booking invoice usage, and recording payments. Deliberately excludes state-machine decisions — it executes run operations but never decides which state to enter.

patterns.name: Config-struct constructor with Validate()
patterns.description: New(Config) validates all four required dependencies (Adapter, Rater, Handler, Lineage) before returning *Service.
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (*Service, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err } ... }

patterns.name: Every exported method validates its input struct first
patterns.description: All exported methods call in.Validate() as their first action. Input structs implement Validate() and validate sub-fields recursively with wrapped error messages.
patterns.example: func (s *Service) CreateRatedRun(ctx context.Context, in CreateRatedRunInput) (...) { if err := in.Validate(); err != nil { return ..., err } }

patterns.name: CreditAllocationMode enum controls credit allocation behaviour
patterns.description: CreditAllocationNone/Exact/Available passed into CreateRatedRun determines whether credits are allocated, and whether exact-match is required.
patterns.example: CreditAllocation: CreditAllocationExact // allocates exact run total; error if ledger cannot satisfy

patterns.name: Lineage created immediately after credit realizations
patterns.description: createRunCreditRealizations always calls lineage.CreateInitialLineages and lineage.PersistCorrectionLineageSegments right after adapter.CreateRunCreditRealization — never split these calls.

patterns.name: Handler callbacks for ledger side-effects
patterns.description: Ledger writes (credit allocation, payment authorization, settlement, invoice usage accrual) go through usagebased.Handler callbacks (OnCreditsOnlyUsageAccrued, OnInvoiceUsageAccrued, OnPaymentAuthorized, OnPaymentSettled) — never call ledger directly.

patterns.name: ensureDetailedLinesLoadedForRating before creating a new run
patterns.description: CreateRatedRun calls ensureDetailedLinesLoadedForRating to lazy-fetch prior run detailed lines from the adapter if any run lacks them — required by the rating package's PriorRuns validation.

key_file_guides.role: Defines Service struct, Config, New constructor, and CreditAllocationMode enum.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Service intentionally has no state-machine logic — never add status transition decisions here.

key_file_guides.role: CreateRatedRun: full run creation flow — rate, validate totals, persist run, persist detailed lines, optionally allocate credits.
key_file_guides.watch_for: runTotals must be rounded with CurrencyCalculator before negative-total check and before UpdateRealizationRun.

key_file_guides.role: ReconcileCredits (delta allocation/correction) and CorrectAllCredits for zeroing out run credits. Uses lineage.LoadActiveSegmentsByRealizationID before generating corrections.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ReconcileCredits rounds TargetAmount and currentAmount before computing delta — never compare unrounded decimals.

key_file_guides.role: Private allocate() helper and createRunCreditRealizations() that pairs credit creation with lineage writes.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never call adapter.CreateRunCreditRealization without the two subsequent lineage calls — they must be atomic from a business logic standpoint.

key_file_guides.role: BookAccruedInvoiceUsage: books the invoice usage accrual via handler then persists AccruedUsage. Short-circuits on zero-total lines.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Empty TransactionGroupID from handler is an error — enforce this check before persisting.

key_file_guides.role: BookInvoicedPaymentAuthorized and SettleInvoicedPayment: authorize/settle payment via handler then persist via adapter.
key_file_guides.watch_for: State guards in Validate() (payment already authorized, mismatched line ID, already settled) must be preserved in any new payment methods.

key_file_guides.role: mapRatingResultToRunDetailedLines (rating->domain mapping) and PersistRunDetailedLines (upsert to adapter).
key_file_guides.watch_for: TaxConfig must be cloned via cfg.Clone() — never assign the charge pointer directly or mutations will bleed across runs.

anti_patterns: Making state-machine decisions (status transitions, trigger selection) inside this package — those belong in the usagebased charge service layer above.
Calling ledger adapters directly — all ledger side-effects must flow through usagebased.Handler callbacks.
Splitting createRunCreditRealizations from its lineage calls — CreateInitialLineages and PersistCorrectionLineageSegments must always follow credit creation.
Comparing alpacadecimal.Decimal values before rounding with CurrencyCalculator — rounding order matters for billing correctness.
Assigning charge.Intent.TaxConfig pointer directly into DetailedLine — always clone with cfg.Clone() to prevent mutation across run boundaries.

decisions.decision: Service owns run mechanics but not state-machine decisions
decisions.rationale: Separating the 'how to execute a run' (rating, persistence, credit math) from 'when to execute a run' (state transitions) keeps this package testable without a full state machine and prevents accidental coupling of lifecycle logic to run mechanics.

decisions.decision: Handler interface for all ledger side-effects
decisions.rationale: Ledger calls (credit allocation, payment, invoice accrual) are externally provided via usagebased.Handler so this package compiles and tests without a real ledger implementation, and the ledger integration point is a single seam per operation type.

decisions.decision: CreditAllocationMode enum instead of boolean flags
decisions.rationale: Three distinct allocation semantics (none, exact, available) cannot be expressed cleanly with a boolean; an enum with Validate() catches invalid values at construction time and self-documents caller intent.

code_examples.scenario: Create a final realization run with exact credit allocation
